chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No.S-160 of 2025 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge. Hearing of Case 1.For orders on office objections 2.For orders on CMA 503/25 3.For hearing of main case 11-08-2025 Atta Muhammad, petiotner is present in person. M/s Saeed Ahmed Wassan, Assistant A.G and Muhammad Raza Katohar, APG a/w Inspector Mashooq, SHO, P.S, Gambat, Ghulam Hyder, SHO, P.S, Khuhra and SIP Gulzar on behalf of SSP, Khairpur and SIP Muhammad Ali of P.S, Baharo. **** Mr. Muhammad Shahid Shaikh, Advocate files Vakalatnama on behalf of private respondents No.9&10. Learned AAG and APG file statements on behalf of respondents No.3 to 8, which are taken on record and copies whereof are provided to the petiotner. In their statements, official respondents have submitted that in fact, there is a dispute between the petitioner and private respondents over landed property, which is purely of civil nature. However, they have categorically denied to have caused any sort of harassment or pressurized the petitioner in any manner, without due course of law. Learned Law Officers submit that the official respondents shall conduct themselves strictly in accordance with law. After such statement, the petitioner being satisfied seeks disposal of instant petition. Since the right to protection and security of every citizen, including the petitioner, is a fundamental right enshrined and safeguarded under the Constitution of Pakistan, it is incumbent upon the official respondents to uphold this right without discrimination. Therefore, the official respondents are hereby directed to act strictly in accordance with the law and ensure that the petitioner as well as private respondents are provided with adequate protection as

Since the right to protection and security of every citizen, including the petitioner, is a fundamental right enshrined and safeguarded under the Constitution of Pakistan, it is incumbent upon the official respondents to uphold this right without discrimination. Therefore, the official respondents are hereby directed to act strictly in accordance with the law and ensure that the petitioner as well as private respondents are provided with adequate protection as 2 guaranteed by the Constitution and relevant legal provisions. However, it is made clear that this order shall not come in the way either in criminal, civil or family cases, if pending between both the parties before the competent Courts of law. Accordingly, instant petition is disposed of along with listed application in the above terms. JUDGE Ahmad
